purpose: Migrate the five existing themes (calm, playful, lego, minecraft, flower) from theme.ts's inline createTheme() calls into pure-data manifests under app/src/themes/<id>/manifest.ts, wire a committed registry.generated.ts stub, flip themes/index.ts from S1's no-op pass-through into the real public API, and flip lib/theme.ts into a thin re-export. This is the story that makes the byte-identical parity guarantee (AC-PARITY-1/AC-PARITY-2) real for the first time.

public_api.name: manifest (per theme folder)
public_api.description: Pure-data manifest, migrated field-for-field from theme.ts's pre-refactor createTheme() inputs + APP_THEMES entry + useThemeStyles.ts's per-visualStyle treatment blocks + BANNER_SVG. No Chakra import, no functions, import type only for ThemeManifest.

public_api.name: AppThemeName (registry.generated.ts)
public_api.description: Folder-name-derived union; the single source of truth other modules (types/index.ts) now import from, instead of hardcoding.

public_api.name: APP_THEMES (registry.generated.ts)
public_api.description: Pure registry map, order-sorted (calm=1..flower=5), computed at eval time in the committed stub via the hand-written order-sort helper (Implementation Constraint 8). NOT the same object as themes/index.ts's exported APP_THEMES (see design_notes).

public_api.name: THEME_FONTS (registry.generated.ts)
public_api.description: buildThemeFonts(order-sorted manifests); consumed directly by _document.tsx in S4 (this story does not touch _document.tsx).

public_api.name: isAppThemeName / normalizeThemeName / getThemeDefinition / getChakraTheme / DEFAULT_THEME_NAME (themes/index.ts, re-exported by lib/theme.ts)
public_api.description: getChakraTheme(id: AppThemeName) is now the real, registry-backed implementation: id -> APP_THEMES[id] (registry, raw ThemeManifest) -> buildChakraTheme.ts's manifest-keyed getChakraTheme(manifest) (aliased internally to avoid a name collision), cached/referentially-stable via buildChakraTheme.ts's manifest.id-keyed Map.

public_api.name: APP_THEMES (themes/index.ts, re-exported by lib/theme.ts)
public_api.description: The PUBLIC, compat-augmented map (see the transitional bridge design note below) — distinct from registry.generated.ts's own APP_THEMES export of the same name. Built once at module load (Object.fromEntries over the registry's raw manifests), so every lookup is referentially stable.

public_api.name: AppThemeDefinition (themes/index.ts)
public_api.signature: Omit<ThemeManifest,'id'> & { id: AppThemeName; visualStyle: ThemeVisualStyle; labelKey; descriptionKey; backgroundImage? }
public_api.description: TRANSITIONAL compat type — see design_notes. Converges to the literal `= ThemeManifest` alias architecture.md's Registry seam contract describes once S4 removes the four deprecated fields.

public_api.name: ThemeVisualStyle (themes/index.ts, re-exported by lib/theme.ts)
public_api.description: Kept alive only for the transitional bridge; useThemeStyles.ts still imports this type unchanged.

public_api.name: lib/theme.ts
public_api.description: No inline theme object definitions remain (AC-STRUCT-2 for S3, already true after this story).

public_api.name: types/index.ts's AppThemeName
public_api.signature: import type { AppThemeName } from '@/src/themes/registry.generated'; export type { AppThemeName };
public_api.description: Was a hardcoded literal union; now derived from the registry (architecture.md Boundary Rules: types -> registry.generated).

owned_data: the folder-name-derived AppThemeName union + order-sorted APP_THEMES + deduped THEME_FONTS (registry.generated.ts)
the id -> manifest -> ChakraTheme bridge + the transitional AppThemeDefinition compat shape (themes/index.ts)
dependencies_allowed: manifests -> ThemeManifest type only (app/src/themes/schema.ts, import type)
registry.generated.ts -> manifests (static value import), schema.ts (type only), fontUnion.ts (buildThemeFonts value import)
dependencies_forbidden: any manifest.ts -> @chakra-ui/react, buildChakraTheme.ts, or treatments/* (manifests are serializable pure data)

any client-bundle-reachable module (manifests, registry.generated.ts, themes/index.ts) -> a RUNTIME import of schema.ts (AC-BOUND-1 zod boundary; all schema imports in this story's new files are `import type`)
useThemeStyles.ts, ThemeIcon.tsx, profile.tsx, _document.tsx, i18n.ts edits (all S4/out of scope for this story)
test_targets: app/tests/unit/theme.test.ts (pre-existing, unmodified, still green against the new registry-backed lib/theme.ts)
app/tests/unit/themes/index.test.ts (pre-existing S1 referential-identity test, unmodified, still green)
design_notes: TRANSITIONAL COMPAT BRIDGE (the story's #1 risk, per the story brief). architecture.md's Registry seam contract states `AppThemeDefinition = ThemeManifest` (a plain alias) as the epic's END STATE. That is only true once S4 migrates useThemeStyles.ts (reads activeThemeDefinition.visualStyle/.contentSurface), ThemeIcon.tsx (reads .visualStyle), and profile.tsx (reads .labelKey/.descriptionKey/.backgroundImage off every entry in APP_THEMES) off those fields. None of those three files are touched by this story. A naive `AppThemeDefinition = ThemeManifest` today would break all three, both at the type level (fields wouldn't exist) and at runtime. This story's AppThemeDefinition is therefore `Omit<ThemeManifest,'id'> & { id: AppThemeName; visualStyle; labelKey; descriptionKey; backgroundImage? }` — every extra field is *derived* from the manifest (never independently authored data), so there is exactly one source of truth: visualStyle <- treatments.card via a hand-maintained ELEVATION_TO_VISUAL_STYLE reverse map (card/button/nav share one elevation name for all five migrated themes, so reading .card is equivalent to reading any of the three); labelKey/descriptionKey <- id (id already equals the historical i18n key for all five themes; descriptionKey appends the literal 'Description' suffix i18n.ts already uses); backgroundImage <- colors.backgroundImage. contentSurface needed NO derivation at all: ThemeManifest.contentSurface (S1's schema.ts) is already the exact same top-level optional boolean the pre-refactor AppThemeDefinition had — it was never nested under a 'behavior' object in this codebase's actual schema (the story brief's prose mentioned 'nested behavior.contentSurface' as a generic possibility, but S1's committed schema.ts already placed it at the top level, so no nesting/derivation was needed or introduced). S4 removes the four deprecated fields once the three consumers migrate; AppThemeDefinition then collapses to the literal `= ThemeManifest` alias with no further code change beyond deleting those fields and the reverse-map table.

TWO 'APP_THEMES' EXPORTS BY DESIGN, NOT COLLISION. registry.generated.ts exports a pure Record<AppThemeName, ThemeManifest> named APP_THEMES (matches architecture.md's Registry seam contract literally — this is what S3's themes-validation.test.ts metadata-freshness check (Implementation Constraint 7) diffs against, since that check explicitly compares against 'what the committed registry.generated.ts exports', not against index.ts's transformed map). themes/index.ts separately exports its OWN APP_THEMES, of the compat-augmented AppThemeDefinition shape, built by mapping over the registry's raw entries. This is the one that flows through lib/theme.ts to profile.tsx/theme.test.ts/useMoodTheme.tsx and must carry labelKey/descriptionKey/etc. for those un-migrated consumers to keep compiling and behaving identically. The two never collide because they live in different modules and nothing imports both under the same local name.

id -> manifest -> getChakraTheme(manifest) bridge, no name collision. themes/index.ts imports buildChakraTheme.ts's manifest-keyed getChakraTheme under the alias getCachedChakraTheme, then defines its OWN getChakraTheme(id: AppThemeName) that resolves id -> registry.generated's raw APP_THEMES[id] -> getCachedChakraTheme(manifest). This realizes the CARRIED S1 GUARANTEE (getchakratheme-id-manifest-bridge) exactly as S1's buildChakraTheme.ts design note anticipated: 'the id -> manifest -> getChakraTheme(manifest) composition is wired there once the registry exists (S2+)'. Referential stability is inherited for free from buildChakraTheme.ts's manifest.id-keyed cache — no new caching logic was needed in index.ts for getChakraTheme itself (the compat AppThemeDefinition map, by contrast, IS built eagerly once at module load specifically so getThemeDefinition's referential-stability property doesn't depend on a second cache).

iconSet derivation correctness (not directly tested by this story's ACs, but load-bearing for S4). ThemeIcon.tsx's pre-refactor ICON_MAP has only 'pixel'/'toy'/'default' keys (no 'rounded' or 'floral' key), so under the old `iconSet[vs] ?? iconSet.default` fallback: calm(soft)->default/line, playful(rounded)->default/line (no 'rounded' key), lego(toy)->toy/filled, minecraft(pixel)->pixel/pixel, flower(floral)->default/line (no 'floral' key). Each manifest's treatments.iconSet was set to match this exactly (calm/playful/flower: 'line'; lego: 'filled'; minecraft: 'pixel') rather than naively assuming a 1:1 name mapping, so S4's ThemeIcon.tsx rewiring onto treatments.iconSet won't silently change which icon set flower/playful render with.

Banner SVGs copied verbatim (including original multi-line whitespace) from useThemeStyles.ts's BANNER_SVG record into each manifest's treatments.banner. useThemeStyles.ts itself is unmodified in this story and continues to read its own internal BANNER_SVG constant — nothing consumes manifest.treatments.banner yet. This story only ensures the data is correctly staged for S4's rewiring; getting it right now (rather than deferring) avoids a second full-fidelity transcription pass later.

shape/fontSizes/fontLoad omitted (left undefined) wherever the pre-refactor theme did not set them (calm/playful: no radii key at all; calm/playful/lego/flower: no fontSizes key). This exactly reproduces extendTheme's merge behavior against Chakra's own defaults, which is what the S0 baseline actually captured (radii/fontSizes ARE present in the baseline for every theme, including calm, because extendTheme always merges in Chakra's default scale regardless of whether the override provided one) — verified directly against theme-baseline.generated.ts before authoring the manifests.
